zh.lifeSummary: Thomas de Davenport於1343年出生。他和Margeret Venables至少有1個女兒。他去世於1390年享年47歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Thomas de Davenport于1343年出生。他和Margeret Venables至少有1个女儿。他去世于1390年享年47岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Davenport
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Cheshire): habitational name from a place in Cheshire named Davenport, from the Dane river (apparently named with a Celtic cognate of Middle Welsh dafnu ‘to drop, to trickle’) + Old English port ‘market town’.

Irish: in Tipperary, this is an English surname adopted by bearers of Munster Gaelic Ó Donndubhartaigh ‘descendant of Donndubhartach’, a personal name composed of the elements donn ‘brown-haired man’ or ‘chieftain’ + dubh ‘black’ + artach ‘nobleman’.
History: John Davenport (died 1670) arrived in Boston, MA, in 1637. He came of an English Cheshire family associated with Capesthorne Hall, near Macclesfield.

variants.name: Devenport
meanings: English and Irish (Lancashire): variant of Davenport . The spelling Devonport is found in Ireland and it may have been this form which gave rise to Devenport.

variants.name: Dunworth
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Eamon, Niall.
Irish: adopted in Ireland by bearers of Gaelic Ó Donndubhartaigh (see Davenport 2).
English: perhaps a variant of Dungworth, see Dunsworth .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas de Davenport was born in 1343. He had at least 1 daughter with Margeret Venables. He died in 1390, at the age of 47.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas de Davenport est né en 1343. Il a eu au moins 1 fille avec Margeret Venables. Il est décédé en 1390, à l’âge de 47 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas de Davenport wurde 1343 geboren. Er hatte mit Margeret Venables mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1390 im Alter von 47 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas de Davenport è nato il nel 1343. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia con Margeret Venables. Morì nel 1390, all'età di 47 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas de Davenportは1343年に生まれた。彼はMargeret Venablesとの間に少なくとも1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1390年に47歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas de Davenport(은)는 1343년 태어났다. 그는 Margeret Venables(와)과 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1390년 47세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Thomas de Davenport nasceu em 1343. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filha com Margeret Venables. Ele faleceu em 1390, com 47 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas de Davenport родился в 1343 г. У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь от Margeret Venables. Он умер в 1390 г. в возрасте 47 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Thomas de Davenport nació en 1343. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hija con Margeret Venables. Murió en 1390, a la edad de 47 años.
